title: Ginny and the Birth of Bonney
description: On pages 7–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #7, Saturn, Bonney, Unknown person #8, Unknown person #9, Kuma as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Why when humans are told not do something... they do it anyway?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No, it's impossible. Some kind of force is holding us down!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If it's not science...it has to be a devil fruit....!

dialogue.speaker: Bonney
dialogue.text: Oh, Kuma? He was born a slave...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A remnant of a tribe of people that committed a grave crime...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Ever since I was a child...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I always wanted to be a hero, one who brought free- Dom to those who are suff- Fering

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Just like the warrior of liberation, Nika!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He would dance into action with a smile on his face...!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: 47 years ago in the South Blue sorbet Kingdom

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Here he is, our little boy!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oh, about his blood? We won't let any information about this get out.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The govern- Ment had eyes in the hospitals too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I'm so sorry! iI'm so sorry!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm the only one with buccanear blood running through my veins!

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 44 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
